ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for lion's mane mushroom cheesy cutlet specifically. FOODON_03315595 (mushroom food product) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP for processed mushroom products is the closest parent. No specific code exists for lion's mane mushroom cutlet with cheese.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 450 g, comprising two lion's mane mushroom cheesy cutlet patties with a portion of mashed potatoes, baked beans, mixed salad, and brown sauce.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated composite from USDA FoodData Central: Mushrooms, lion's mane, raw (FDC ID 2346406); Cheese, cheddar (FDC ID 328637); Mashed potatoes, home-prepared (FDC ID 172030); Baked beans, canned (FDC ID 173744); Mixed salad greens (FDC ID 169247); Brown gravy sauce (FDC ID 173580); Breadcrumbs, dry (FDC ID 172822)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry exists for lion's mane mushroom cheesy cutlet in any approved primary source. Values are estimated by weighted composite of USDA FoodData Central entries for the constituent components: lion's mane mushroom (~30%), cheese (~10%), breadcrumb coating (~10%), mashed potato (~25%), baked beans (~15%), mixed salad (~5%), brown sauce (~5%). Energy and macros are calculated from these proportions. This is a medium-confidence estimate.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 (Atkinson et al.) — mixed meal with potato, legumes, and breaded component analogous estimate; potato GI ~52 (boiled/mashed), baked beans GI ~40, breaded coating GI ~55
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for lion's mane mushroom cheesy cutlet. GI estimated as weighted composite: mashed potato (GI 52, University of Sydney GI Database), baked beans (GI 40, University of Sydney GI Database), breaded coating analogous to white bread-based coating (GI 55, University of Sydney GI Database). Weighted by carbohydrate contribution of each component. Composite GI estimated at 52. GL per serving = (52 × 83.25 g carbohydrate per 450 g serving) / 100 = 43.3. gi_category set to 'low' as GI 52 ≤ 55.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~380 mg/100 g; ~1710 mg per full serving) from brown sauce, baked beans, and cheese
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be cautious of the high sodium load in this dish, particularly from the brown sauce and baked beans; requesting reduced sauce or choosing a smaller portion can help limit sodium intake to within the recommended 2000 mg/day ceiling.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~43 GL per full serving) driven by mashed potato and baked beans carbohydrate content
type2_diabetes.guidance: The full double-patty serving carries a high glycemic load (~43) despite a low GI of 52; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consider a half-portion or substitute the mashed potato with a lower-GI side such as steamed vegetables to reduce postprandial glucose excursion.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from cheese (~2.4 g/100 g; ~10.8 g per serving) and moderate sodium load
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The cheese component contributes meaningful saturated fat (~10.8 g per full serving), which approaches the recommended daily limit of 20 g for cardiovascular risk management; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size and consider requesting reduced cheese or a lighter sauce.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Lion's Mane Mushroom Cheesy Cutlet (Double Patty) is a plant-forward restaurant dish served at Flavours by Saute at Funan Mall, Singapore. It features two pan-fried, breaded patties made from lion's mane mushroom (Hericium erinaceus), a prized culinary and medicinal fungus known for its distinctive shaggy white appearance and meat-like fibrous texture that closely mimics the mouthfeel of a traditional meat cutlet. The patties are filled or topped with melted cheese, creating a rich, savoury, umami-forward flavour profile with a crispy golden breadcrumb exterior and a tender, juicy interior. The dish is served as a complete plated meal alongside creamy mashed potatoes, baked beans in tomato sauce, a fresh mixed salad, and brown sauce. Nutritionally, the dish provides moderate protein (~6.2 g/100 g) from the mushroom and cheese, moderate carbohydrates (~18.5 g/100 g) primarily from the potato and beans, and moderate fat (~5.8 g/100 g) largely from the cheese and frying medium. The dietary fibre content (~2.8 g/100 g) is notable given the mushroom and legume components. The dish is vegetarian but not vegan due to cheese and egg in the coating. Clinically, the full double-patty serving carries a moderate glycemic load (~43) and a high sodium load (~1710 mg), making portion control important for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. Lion's mane mushroom has been studied for potential neuroprotective and immunomodulatory properties, though these are not established as clinical dietary claims.

confidence_reason: No direct nutritional entry exists for this specific restaurant dish in any approved primary source. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted composite of USDA FoodData Central entries for the constituent ingredients. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database values for the dominant carbohydrate-contributing components (mashed potato, baked beans, breaded coating). Confidence is medium rather than low because the component sources are individually well-supported in USDA FoodData Central and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entries for the constituent components of this dish and cannot be reliably estimated. All other -1 fields have been replaced with estimated values where component data was available. The food_name in the input ('Lion Mane Cheeeezy Cutlet (Double Patty)') contains a non-standard spelling ('Cheeeezy') which is a stylised menu name; the canonical English name has been normalised to 'Lion's Mane Mushroom Cheesy Cutlet (Double Patty)'. The food_group in the input was listed as 'Grains and staples' which is incorrect for a mushroom-based meat alternative cutlet dish; corrected to 'Meat and alternatives'. The cuisine_tags in the input (Ramen, Pizza, Pasta) appear to be platform-level restaurant tags for Flavours by Saute and do not describe this specific dish; they have been disregarded for this record. Halal status is not confirmed as the field dataing does not carry a MUIS halal certification; is_halal set to false. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (52 × 83.25) / 100 = 43.3, where carbohydrate per 450 g serving = 18.5 × 4.5 = 83.25 g.

improvement_actions_taken: Normalised food_name from stylised menu spelling 'Lion Mane Cheeeezy Cutlet (Double Patty)' to canonical 'Lion's Mane Mushroom Cheesy Cutlet (Double Patty)'.
Corrected food_group from 'Grains and staples' to 'Meat and alternatives' to accurately reflect mushroom-based meat alternative classification.
Added full multilingual local_names and aliases for Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi as required for Singapore cuisine context.
Constructed weighted composite nutrient estimate from USDA FoodData Central component entries with transparent methodology.
Derived GI as analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database values for dominant carbohydrate components.
Documented vitamin_d_mcg = -1 sentinel value with explicit reason in notes.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases list for perfect consistency before finalising.
Set gi_category to 'low' correctly for GI 52 (≤ 55 threshold).
Used only 'exact', 'closest_parent', or 'none' for all ontology match_type fields.
